content.cn_text: 你将听到两个学生，Ramil和Kirsten，讨论在一个建筑会议上发表一篇论文。
content.en_text: You will hear two students, Ramil and Kirsten, discussing presenting a paper at an architecture conference.

content.cn_text: 拉米尔:嗨，柯尔斯顿。你听说过今年年底在牛津举行的建筑会议吗?
content.en_text: Ramil : Hi, Kirsten. Have you heard about that architecture conference in Oxford at the end of the year?

content.cn_text: 克里斯滕:对。我在布告栏上看到了传单。因为这是我的最后一年。我应该试着写一篇论文。但我不知道该怎么做!
content.en_text: Kirsten : Yeah. I saw the leaflet on the noticeboard . As it's my final year. 1 ought to try giving a paper. But I've got no idea how to go about it!

content.cn_text: 拉米尔:我觉得你应该试试。我去年做过一次。这很简单。首先。你需要知道会议的主题是什么——你知道，他们涵盖了什么话题。你可以在网站上查一下。你需要提交一篇属于他们给你的类别之一的论文。
content.en_text: Ramil : I think you should go for it. 1 did one last year. It's quite straightforward . First of all. you need to sec what the conference themes are – you know, what topics they are covering. You can do that by looking it up on the website . You need to submit a paper that falls into one of the categories they give you.

content.cn_text: 克里斯滕:哦。这也许会给我一些启发。
content.en_text: Kirsten : Oh. that may give me some ideas.

content.cn_text: 拉米尔:然后，当你这样做的时候，你还应该看看如何提交论文的信息——如果你喜欢的话，也可以是“规则”，比如长度。遵循这些是很重要的。
content.en_text: Ramil : Then, while you're doing that, you should also have a look at the information on how to submit your paper – the 'rules', if yon like, such as the length. It's important you follow those.

content.cn_text: 克里斯滕:我明白了。那么我想下一阶段就是开始写下来。我想借此机会提出一些未来的工作，但我明白这必须基于
content.en_text: Kirsten : I see. Then 1 suppose the next stage is to start writing it up. I'd like to use it as an opportunity to propose some future work, but I understand it must be based on

content.cn_text: 当前工作。仍然有很多选择。做一些我更熟悉的事情是有意义的。
content.en_text: current work. Still there's plenty to choose from. It makes sense to do something that I'm more familiar with.

content.cn_text: 拉米尔:对，另一件事是，当你写下来的时候。然后回头仔细查看你的数据，并确保你以一种标准的格式呈现它。记住，人们在听的时候必须很快地吸收信息——不要把它弄得太复杂。
content.en_text: Ramil : Yes, – and the other thing is, when you've written it up. then go back and look at your data carefully and make certain that you've presented it in a format that is standard for your subicit. Remember people have to absorb information very quickly while they're listening – don't make it too complicated .

content.cn_text: 克尔斯滕:好吧，我估计我要花一个月的时间来解决这个问题。然后，我想，在我把它寄给会议组织者之前，我要做的下一件事是把整个东西交给活动官员，让他看一遍，看看是否有意义，是否没问题。
content.en_text: Kirsten : OK, well, I reckon that'll take me about a month to get that sorted. Then, the next thing 1 have to do, I guess, before I send it off to the conference organiser , is give the whole thing to the events officer so that he can look through it and see if it all makes sense and is OK.

content.cn_text: 拉米尔:对。记得提醒他在路上，这样他就可以把它安排在他的时间表里了!这样你就真的完成了。之后你所要做的就是通读一遍，找出你需要做出的改变。最后你就可以提交了。你可以在网上做。
content.en_text: Ramil : Yeah. Remember to warn him that it's en route so he can fit it into his schedule !Then you're done really. All you have to do after that is to go through it and sort out any changes you need lo make. Then finally you can submit it. You can do that online.

content.cn_text: 柯尔斯顿:呸!好。那我就等着听吧。那要花多长时间?
content.en_text: Kirsten : Phew! Good. Then I just wait to hear I suppose. How long docs that take?

content.cn_text: 拉米尔:看情况，通常是六周。当你听到，如果你的论文被接受了，那么，在那个阶段，你应该给他们一个清单，列出你在演讲时需要的任何技术设备屏幕或视频播放器之类的东西。
content.en_text: Ramil : Depends – but usually about six weeks. When you hear, if your paper has been accepted , then, at that stage, it's worth giving them a list of any technical things you'll need when you actually give the talk a screen or video players or that sort of thing.

content.cn_text: 克尔斯滕:好吧，但那还有很长的路要走。我知道，如果我的论文被接受了，那么，在那个阶段，我必须给他们一段关于我自己和我的学术背景的简短文字，这样他们就可以把它放在小册子里。终于出名了!
content.en_text: Kirsten : OK – but that's a long way off. And I know that, if my paper is accepted, then, at that stage, I have to give them a short text about myself and my academic background ,so that they can put it in the brochure . Famous, at last!

content.cn_text: 拉米尔:对。
content.en_text: Ramil : Yeah.

content.cn_text: 克尔斯滕:好吧。如果我要完成这篇论文，我必须把一些事情整理好。
content.en_text: Kirsten: Right – well. I've got to get a couple of things sorted if I'm going to get this paper completed.

content.cn_text: 拉米尔:你有足够的数据吗?克尔斯滕:可能吧。我想再强调一下。不过，所以我想我应该再发一些调查问卷。我在看安吉拉去年写的论文，她说你需要一百多个样本来确定你的结果。
content.en_text: Ramil : Have you got enough data? Kirsten : Possibly. I'd like to reinforce some of it.though, so i thought I'd send out some more questionnaires i . i was looking at that thesis that Angela wrote last year and she said you need a sample of over a hundred to be sure of your results.

content.cn_text: 拉米尔:我想今年的一些研究生也在做一些和你一样的关于建筑的研究。你为什么不跟他们谈谈?
content.en_text: Ramil : I think some of this year's postgraduates are doing some of the same stuff as you on buildings. Why don't you talk to them?

content.cn_text: 克尔斯滕:我最终会感到困惑。对我来说，去铁路桥旁的那个地方看看他们是如何建造新工厂的会更有用。我设法
content.en_text: Kirsten : I'll end up getting confused. It would be more useful for me to actually go out to that site by the rail bridge to see how they're building the new factory. I managed to

content.cn_text: 我找到了伦敦大学的巴奈特教授，他说我应该出去拍照。我很忙，但我得挤出时间来。不管怎样，你呢……
content.en_text: get hold of Professor Barnett at London University and he said I should go out and take pictures. I'm pretty busy but I'll have to make time. Anyway, what about you … (fade)…

groups.desc:
Questions 21-28
Complete the flow-chart below.
Write NO MORE THAN TWO WORDS for each answer.

items.answer: the website / website / the internet / internet
items.analysis: 因为文中明确提到可以通过“looking it up on the website”来找到“conference themes”的详细信息，所以答案是“website”或“internet”。
根据题干中的“rules for submission”定位到听力材料中的对应部分，听力中提到需要查看提交规则，其中举例说明了长度（length）这一项。因此答案是“length”。
答案是“current”，因为文中明确提到“Kirsten”希望利用这个机会提出一些未来的工作，但必须基于当前的工作。题干要求填写写论文的内容，而文中这句话清楚地表明了论文必须基于“current work”，所以答案是“current”。

答案是 "format"。文中提到 "look at your data carefully and make certain that you've presented it in a format that is standard for your subject"，这里明确指出数据需要以一种适合的格式展示，这与题干中的 "Check any data is in a suitable ________" 对应。定位词为 "data"，对应的信号词也是 "data"。因此正确答案是 "format"。

这道题目的正确答案是“events officer”。题目要求填写的是提交论文时交给谁检查。在听力原文中，克里斯顿提到了“give the whole thing to the events officer so that he can look through it”，这与“Hand in paper to the”同义，因此得出答案是“events officer”。尽管“send it off to the conference organiser”也出现在原文中，但它只是说明提交给活动官员是提交给会议组织者前的步骤，而不是直接提交对象。通过理解这些同义替换和上下文关系，我们可以明确正确答案。
文中的 "go through it and sort out any changes you need to make" 说明了在提交论文（submit paper）之前，需要进行的步骤是做任何必要的修改（necessary changes），所以答案是“changes”或“revisions”。

第27题的答案是“technical”。在听力材料中，提到“if your paper has been accepted”时，接着说在那个阶段你需要提供一个技术需求的清单，具体提到了“a screen or video players or that sort of thing”，这说明需要填写的是“technical requirements”。因此，通过定位词“accepted”和其在文中对应的信号词，可以找到答案句，并确定答案是“technical”。
因为文中提到，论文被接受后需要提交一份关于自己和学术背景的简短文字，以便放入小册子（brochure）中。所以，第28题的正确答案是“brochure”。“personal profile”在文中对应的是“short text about myself and my academic background”，而这部分文字会被放入“小册子”中，即“brochure”。

groups.title: Which TWO things does Kirsten plan to do to prepare her conference paper?

collect_options.title: Which TWO things does Kirsten plan to do to prepare her conference paper?

list.title: D
list.content: contact a Professor at another university

items.analysis: Kirsten计划做两件事来准备她的会议论文：发送更多问卷和实地考察建筑工地。这两个计划分别对应选项B和E。Kirsten明确表示她想要"加强一些数据"，并打算通过发送更多问卷来实现这一目标。同时，她认为亲自去工地查看新工厂的建设情况会更有用。这两个行动都是她为了完成论文而计划进行的，符合题目要求的"计划做的事"和"准备会议论文"。
Kirsten计划做两件事来准备她的会议论文：发送更多问卷和实地考察建筑工地。这两个计划分别对应选项B和E。Kirsten明确表示她想要"加强一些数据"，并打算通过发送更多问卷来实现这一目标。同时，她认为亲自去工地查看新工厂的建设情况会更有用。这两个行动都是她为了完成论文而计划进行的，符合题目要求的"计划做的事"和"准备会议论文"。
